justification: Both invariant violations explicitly identified and named (BR-1: single carrier per shipment, BR-2: supervisor-only reassignment). Escalation produced with specific reference to both violated invariants. No acceptance criteria written (acceptance_criteria array is empty). Options proposed (A-D) are clearly labeled as proposals, not accepted requirements. Confidence level is 45, well within the <=55 threshold.

observations.text: Both invariants identified with precision: BR-1 (exactly one carrier) and BR-2 (supervisor-only reassignment) are named, justified, and referenced throughout
Acceptance criteria array is empty — Bird refused to write criteria for a contradicted feature
Escalation is formal and structured with four concrete options (A-D), each clearly labeled as a proposal requiring stakeholder alignment
Confidence at 45 with well-reasoned justification citing unresolvable contradictions
Edge cases are exceptional — covers liability ambiguity, billing reconciliation, customer tracking fracture, and failure recovery contradictions
Identified additional downstream risks (legal/compliance, billing, customer experience) beyond the two core invariant violations
Recommendation favors preserving invariants (Option C: supervisor-approved system-suggested split) rather than bulldozing through with the feature as specified
Correctly identified 'sub-shipment' as a new concept not in the current domain model, flagging it as requiring formal domain evolution
